category_title: Branching tree with three independent rotary branches
prompt: A three-branch rotary fixture. A central mast carries three radial arms on separate hub blocks at staggered heights, with each arm ending in a small pad or fork. Each arm has its own revolute joint about a local hub axis with about 120 degrees of travel, and the branches should be spaced so their motion envelopes stay visually separate.
label: Branching tree with three independent rotary branches Medium 4

category_title: Branching tree with two independent rotary branches
prompt: A two-branch rotary fixture. A central spine carries two side-supported branch arms on separate hubs, one higher and one lower, with rigid mounting pads at the ends. The two branches are independent revolute joints about parallel axes with about 120 degrees of travel each.
label: Branching tree with two independent rotary branches Medium 4

prompt: A cantilever articulated arm for bench tooling. A base column carries a shoulder link, an elbow link, and a short wrist tool plate with clear clevis supports. Shoulder and elbow are revolute joints about parallel horizontal axes with roughly 120 degrees and 135 degrees of travel, and the wrist rolls about the tool axis roughly 90 degrees each way.
label: Cantilever articulated arm Medium 4

prompt: A coaxial rotary stack. Nested circular stages sit on a common vertical shaft with a base turntable, a middle ring, and a top flange. The base, middle, and top stages are separate revolute joints about the shared vertical axis, with the lower stage continuous and the upper stages limited to about 120 degrees each way.
label: Coaxial rotary stack Medium 4

prompt: A dual-finger mechanism with two independent serial finger chains on a shared palm block. Each finger has three tapered phalanx links joined by pin knuckles and mounted side by side on separate base pivots. Each finger chain has three revolute joints bending in one plane, and the two fingers should be mechanically independent rather than coupled.
label: Dual independent finger chains Medium 4

prompt: A dual-column lift carriage. Two vertical guide columns are tied by a base and top crosshead, with a rectangular carriage riding between them on four guide blocks. One central prismatic lift joint moves the carriage vertically about 250 mm while the frame stays fixed.
label: Dual-column lift carriage Medium 3

prompt: A compact dual-column lift carriage with twin round posts and a wide tooling plate. The columns are anchored to a heavy base and bridged at the top, while the moving carriage wraps both columns with separate bearing sleeves. The carriage slides prismatically up the column axis about 200 mm, with obvious hard stops at top and bottom.
label: Dual-column lift carriage Medium 4

prompt: A dual-rail gantry axis. Two parallel base rails support a bridge carriage, and a small center truck rides along the bridge beam. The bridge moves prismatically along the rail axis about 300 mm, and the center truck moves prismatically along the orthogonal bridge axis about 150 mm.
label: Dual-rail gantry axis Medium 3

prompt: A wide gantry positioning axis. A fixed base carries two long rails, a stiff crossbeam rides on them as the first carriage, and a short tool truck slides along the crossbeam. The gantry carriage is prismatic along the base about 250 mm and the tool truck is prismatic across the beam about 120 mm.
label: Dual-rail gantry axis Medium 4

prompt: A finger-like phalanx chain. A base knuckle carries three tapered rigid phalanx links with clear side plates and pin joints. The three revolute joints bend in one plane, with the base joint about 0 to 90 degrees and the distal joints about 0 to 75 degrees.
label: Finger-like phalanx chain Medium 4

prompt: A folding arm chain like a compact support arm. A base bracket carries three flat bar links joined in series by offset clevis pivots and ending in a small pad. The chain has three revolute joints folding in one plane so the arm can collapse nearly flat and extend to a straight reach.
label: Folding arm chain Medium 4

prompt: A four-joint revolute chain. Five rigid links are connected in series by evenly spaced hinge blocks, with the root link mounted to a fixed base foot. All four joints are revolute about parallel axes in one plane, each with about 90 degrees of travel each way.
label: Four-joint revolute chain Medium 4

prompt: An open gear train bench assembly. A steel side frame holds three parallel shafts with a large spur gear, an idler, and a smaller output gear, plus a handwheel on the input shaft. Each shaft is a revolute joint about its supported bearing axis, with the gears arranged in one plane and visibly meshed.
label: Gear Assemblies Medium 3

prompt: A right-angle gear assembly. A base bracket carries a vertical input shaft with a bevel gear and a horizontal output shaft with a mating bevel gear, plus a secondary spur reduction on the horizontal shaft. The input and output shafts are separate revolute joints about their supported axes, and every gear should sit on a clear shaft and bearing block.
label: Gear Assemblies Medium 4

prompt: A gimballed joystick mechanism. A square base frame carries an outer pitch yoke, an inner roll yoke, and a central control stick on the gimbal. Pitch and roll are orthogonal revolute joints about intersecting axes with about 20 degrees each way, with the stick rigidly fixed to the inner yoke.
label: Joystick mechanism Medium 2

prompt: A low-profile joystick mechanism for an equipment console. A circular base cup carries a cardan gimbal and a short upright lever capped by a small clamp block. The outer ring pitches about a left-right axis and the inner ring rolls about a front-back axis, both around 18 degrees each way.
label: Joystick mechanism Medium 3

prompt: A heavy-duty joystick mechanism with return-spring posts. A boxed base supports an outer fork, an inner trunnion cradle, and a tall central lever with a boot retaining ring. The two gimbal axes are orthogonal revolute joints about the same control center with roughly 25 degrees of travel each way.
label: Joystick mechanism Medium 4

prompt: A lever chain. A base lug supports three rigid lever bars in series with offset pin joints and a small end tab. All three joints are revolute about parallel axes in one plane, with enough range for the chain to fold compactly and extend into a shallow arc.
label: Lever chain Medium 4

category_title: Linear carriage with independent rotary end-effector
prompt: A linear carriage with an independent rotary end-effector. A base rail carries a sliding carriage, and the carriage holds a short tool spindle on its own bearing housing. The carriage is prismatic along the rail about 200 mm, and the tool spindle is a separate revolute joint about its local axis with about 180 degrees each way.
label: Linear carriage with independent rotary end-effector Medium 4

prompt: A mast-mounted fork carriage. A vertical mast with two guide rails carries a rectangular fork carriage with twin tines projecting forward. The carriage slides prismatically up the mast about 300 mm while the tines stay rigid to the carriage.
label: Mast-mounted fork carriage Medium 3

prompt: A compact forklift-style mast module. Two upright channels and a top crosshead support a lift carriage with a load backrest and paired forks. One vertical prismatic joint moves the carriage on the mast rails about 250 mm.
label: Mast-mounted fork carriage Medium 4

prompt: A desk-clamped monitor mount shown without a screen. A clamp base carries a lower arm, upper arm, and VESA-style head plate with clear knuckle joints and a pan plate. The shoulder and elbow fold on parallel revolute axes about 120 degrees each way, the head tilts about 20 degrees each way, and the head pans about 45 degrees each way.
label: Monitor mount Medium 3

prompt: A wall-mounted monitor arm shown without a screen. A wall plate supports a primary link, a secondary link, and a compact tilt head with a rectangular mounting frame. The two arm joints fold about vertical pin axes, the head swivels about a vertical axis, and the tilt joint moves about a horizontal axis with roughly 15 to 20 degrees each way.
label: Monitor mount Medium 4

prompt: A multi-segment fold-out arm. A fixed base plate carries four short boxed links in series ending at a small platform bracket. The four revolute joints fold in one plane so the arm packs near the base and extends outward into a straight or slightly arched reach.
label: Multi-segment fold-out arm Medium 4

prompt: An offset-axis rotary stack. A base turntable carries a raised side arm that supports a second rotary stage on a parallel but laterally offset axis, topped by a small flange. The base stage rotates about the main vertical axis and the upper stage rotates about the offset parallel axis, each roughly 120 degrees each way, with the base allowed to go continuous if needed.
label: Offset-axis rotary stack Medium 4

prompt: An opposed twin-slide gripper. A central body carries left and right jaws on mirrored linear slides with guide rails and a fixed mounting plate at the rear. The two jaws are separate prismatic joints along a common closing axis, each with about 40 mm travel toward the center.
label: Opposed twin-slide gripper Medium 3

prompt: An opposed twin-slide gripper with tall jaw fingers. A rectangular center housing supports two parallel guideways and two mirrored jaw carriages that approach a shared pickup zone. Each jaw carriage is a prismatic joint along the same closing axis with about 35 mm of travel.
label: Opposed twin-slide gripper Medium 4

prompt: A two-axis XY positioning stage. A base carries an X slide with a rectangular carriage, and that carriage supports a Y slide turned 90 degrees with a top table. The X and Y stages are prismatic joints along orthogonal horizontal axes, each with about 100 mm of travel each way from center.
label: Orthogonal XY stage Medium 4

prompt: A two-axis XZ positioning stage. A base carries a horizontal X rail and carriage, and the carriage supports a vertical Z slide with a top pad. The X axis is prismatic about 100 mm each way horizontally and the Z axis is prismatic about 150 mm vertically.
label: Orthogonal XZ stage Medium 4

prompt: A two-axis YZ positioning stage. A base frame carries a horizontal Y slide, and the Y carriage supports a vertical Z slide with a small tooling plate. The Y axis is prismatic about 100 mm each way and the Z axis is prismatic upward about 150 mm.
label: Orthogonal YZ stage Medium 4

prompt: A pitch-roll module. A base bracket carries a roll frame, and inside it a pitch cradle supports a small top plate. The outer roll joint rotates about the front-back axis about 45 degrees each way, and the inner pitch joint rotates about the left-right axis about 60 degrees each way.
label: Pitch-roll module Medium 4

prompt: A portal gantry with vertical slide. Two uprights and a top beam form the fixed frame, and a centered carriage runs along the beam with a short Z slide hanging beneath it. The beam carriage is prismatic across the gantry about 250 mm and the Z slide is prismatic vertically about 180 mm.
label: Portal gantry with vertical slide Medium 3

prompt: A compact portal gantry module. Twin side frames support a bridge, a small bridge truck rides under that bridge, and a vertical quill plate hangs from the truck. The truck moves prismatically along the bridge span about 180 mm and the quill moves prismatically down about 120 mm.
label: Portal gantry with vertical slide Medium 4

prompt: A prismatic-revolute chain. A base rail carries a sliding carriage, and the carriage supports a hinged arm on a side clevis. The first joint is prismatic along the rail about 150 mm and the second is a revolute joint at the carriage about 90 degrees each way.
label: Prismatic-revolute chain Medium 4

prompt: A prismatic-revolute-prismatic chain. A base slide carries a pivot bracket, and the pivot bracket holds a second sliding stage aligned with the distal member. The root stage slides about 150 mm, the middle joint rotates about 90 degrees each way, and the distal stage slides about 80 mm along its own axis.
label: Prismatic-revolute-prismatic chain Medium 4

prompt: A prismatic-revolute-revolute chain. A base rail carries a moving carriage, the carriage carries a shoulder link, and a short forelink hangs from an elbow hinge. The root is prismatic about 150 mm, then two serial revolute joints bend in one plane at roughly 90 degrees each way and 75 degrees each way.
label: Prismatic-revolute-revolute chain Medium 4

prompt: A push-pull plunger chain. A fixed housing guides a central plunger, and the plunger tip drives a short hinged lever at the front. The plunger is a prismatic joint along the housing axis about 50 mm, and the front lever is a revolute joint about a transverse pin with about 45 degrees of travel.
label: Push-pull plunger chain Medium 4

prompt: A radial arm with sliding carriage. A base column supports a horizontal arm that sweeps in plan, and a small carriage rides along the arm. The arm rotates about the vertical base axis roughly 120 degrees each way, and the carriage slides prismatically along the arm about 180 mm.
label: Radial arm with sliding carriage Medium 3

prompt: A shop radial-arm module. A short pedestal carries a rigid beam on a rotary shoulder, and a tool carriage travels along the top of that beam on guide rails. The shoulder is a revolute joint about a vertical axis and the carriage is a prismatic joint along the beam with about 150 mm of travel.
label: Radial arm with sliding carriage Medium 4

prompt: A column-mounted radial arm assembly. A vertical post supports a rotary turret, a horizontal beam extends from the turret, and a carriage slides under the beam to a small head plate. The turret rotates about the post axis roughly 90 to 120 degrees each way, and the carriage slides along the beam about 140 mm.
label: Radial arm with sliding carriage Medium 5

prompt: A revolute-prismatic chain. A base bracket supports a swinging arm, and the arm carries a guided slider at its tip. The root joint is revolute about a base pin with about 120 degrees each way, and the distal stage is prismatic along the arm axis about 80 mm.
label: Revolute-prismatic chain Medium 4

prompt: A revolute-prismatic-revolute chain. A base hinge carries a sliding middle link, and the end of that sliding link holds a small tool fork on a pin joint. The root rotates about 120 degrees each way, the middle stage slides about 100 mm, and the distal fork rotates about 75 degrees each way.
label: Revolute-prismatic-revolute chain Medium 4

prompt: A revolute-revolute-prismatic chain. A base bracket carries a shoulder link, an elbow link, and a short sliding tip stage. The first two joints are serial revolutes bending in one plane at about 120 degrees each way and 90 degrees each way, and the tip stage slides about 60 mm along its own axis.
label: Revolute-revolute-prismatic chain Medium 4

prompt: A rotary base with vertical slide. A turntable base carries a vertical column, and the column supports a lifting carriage with a small tool plate. The base rotates about the vertical axis roughly 150 degrees each way, and the carriage slides vertically about 180 mm.
label: Rotary base with vertical slide Medium 4

prompt: A rotary table with tilting trunnion. A base turntable supports a U-shaped trunnion cradle that holds a square work table between its arms. The base rotates about the vertical axis roughly 180 degrees each way, and the trunnion tilts the table about a horizontal axis roughly 60 degrees each way.
label: Rotary table with tilting trunnion Medium 3

prompt: A trunnion table module. A round base stage carries two side cheeks, and a circular workplate sits between them on a transverse shaft. The base is a revolute table about the vertical axis and the workplate is a second revolute joint about the horizontal trunnion axis.
label: Rotary table with tilting trunnion Medium 4

prompt: An elbow arm module. A pedestal supports an upper arm link, a forearm link, and a compact end plate with clear shoulder and elbow hubs. The shoulder and elbow are serial revolute joints about parallel horizontal axes with roughly 120 degrees each way and 135 degrees of forward travel, and the end plate stays rigid to the forearm.
label: Serial elbow arm Medium 4

prompt: A shoulder-elbow-wrist arm. A base pedestal carries an upper arm, a forearm, and a short wrist link ending at a tool flange. The shoulder and elbow are revolute joints about parallel horizontal axes with roughly 120 degrees each way and 135 degrees of forward travel, and the wrist is a third revolute joint near the flange with about 90 degrees each way.
label: Shoulder-elbow-wrist arm Medium 4

prompt: An open rotary shaft module. A fixed bearing block supports one straight shaft with a hub and a flange between two end supports. One continuous revolute joint spins the shaft about its own axis, with the bearings and supports clearly carrying the load.
label: Single continuous rotary shaft Medium 4

prompt: A single prismatic slider. A fixed base rail carries one rectangular carriage with guide blocks and an end plate. One prismatic joint moves the carriage along the rail about 150 mm with clear end stops.
label: Single prismatic slider Medium 4

prompt: A heavy-duty hinge. Two rectangular leaves are joined by alternating knuckles around a central pin, with one leaf fixed to a mounting block. One revolute joint runs along the pin axis with about 0 to 180 degrees of travel.
label: Single revolute hinge Medium 4

prompt: A telescoping boom. A root housing contains two nested rectangular tube stages ending in a small head bracket. The inner stages are prismatic joints along the boom axis with about 250 mm of travel each and enough overlap to stay believable.
label: Telescoping boom Medium 4

prompt: A telescoping mast with pan head. A base sleeve supports two extending mast stages, and the top of the mast carries a small pan table or sensor plate. The mast stages slide prismatically upward about 150 mm each, and the head rotates about the vertical axis roughly 180 degrees each way.
label: Telescoping mast with pan head Medium 3

prompt: A compact telescoping mast head. A grounded outer tube carries two nested lift tubes, and the top tube ends at a shallow rotary platform. The two mast stages are serial prismatic joints on the same vertical axis, and the platform is a revolute pan joint about the mast centerline.
label: Telescoping mast with pan head Medium 4

prompt: A three-stage telescoping slide. Three nested rectangular members extend from a fixed outer sleeve to a small end plate. The middle and inner members are serial prismatic joints along the same axis with about 120 mm of travel each.
label: Three-stage telescoping slide Medium 4

prompt: A TV wall mount shown without any display attached. A wall plate carries a folding two-link arm and a tilt head with a rectangular mounting frame. The shoulder and elbow fold about vertical pin axes roughly 90 degrees each way, the head swivels about 45 degrees each way, and the tilt joint moves about 15 degrees each way.
label: TV wall mount Medium 3

prompt: A compact articulating TV wall mount shown without any display attached. A short wall bracket supports a primary arm, a secondary arm, and a tilting faceplate on separate knuckle blocks. The two arm joints are revolutes about vertical axes, the faceplate swivels slightly about a vertical axis, and the tilt hinge rotates about a horizontal axis.
label: TV wall mount Medium 4

prompt: A two-joint prismatic chain. A fixed outer frame carries a first sliding stage, and that stage carries a second slider aligned with the first. Both joints are prismatic along the same main axis, with about 100 mm of travel on each stage.
label: Two-joint prismatic chain Medium 4

prompt: A two-joint revolute chain. A base bracket carries a first rigid link, which carries a second link ending in a small pad. The two joints are serial revolutes about parallel axes in one plane, each with about 90 degrees of travel each way.
label: Two-joint revolute chain Medium 4

prompt: A two-link folding linkage. A root clevis holds a short primary link and a longer secondary link ending in a rectangular tab. The shoulder and elbow are revolute joints about parallel axes in one plane, with enough range for the linkage to fold close to the base and extend nearly straight.
label: Two-joint revolute chain Medium 5

prompt: A two-stage telescoping slide. A fixed outer sleeve carries one intermediate member and one extending output member with a small end plate. The two stages slide prismatically along the same axis with about 120 mm of travel each.
label: Two-stage telescoping slide Medium 4

prompt: A vertical slide with wrist hinge. A fixed column carries a lifting carriage, and the front of the carriage supports a short wrist plate on a side-supported pin joint. The carriage moves vertically about 180 mm and the wrist rotates about a horizontal axis roughly 90 degrees each way.
label: Vertical slide with wrist hinge Medium 4

prompt: An XYZ cartesian stage. A base supports an X carriage, the X carriage supports a Y stage turned 90 degrees, and the Y stage carries a vertical Z slide with a top table. The X and Y stages are prismatic along orthogonal horizontal axes about 150 mm each, and the Z stage is prismatic vertically about 120 mm.
label: XYZ Cartesian stage Medium 4

prompt: A yaw-pitch-roll wrist. A base ring carries a pitch yoke, and the yoke carries a roll spindle ending in a small tool flange. Yaw rotates about the vertical axis roughly 150 degrees each way, pitch rotates about a horizontal trunnion roughly 60 degrees each way, and roll rotates about the tool axis roughly 180 degrees each way.
label: Yaw-pitch-roll wrist Medium 4
